commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=470863638549b6bd2a5da8c3b2423854c5e43c7c)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a postinstall-time loopback TCP probe and visible restart warning when a listener is detected on the default gateway port, plus an opt-out environment variable.

PR surface: Other +53. Total +53 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect: current docs and source show configured gateway ports are supported, while this PR only probes `18789`. I did not run a live configured-port gateway in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer scope judgment is needed on warning-only postinstall behavior and the new env surface before automation can safely choose the permanent boundary.

target.title: fix(scripts): warn when postinstall detects running gateway with stale module imports
target.body:
## Summary

After `npm install -g openclaw` replaces dist files (upgrade, rollback, or reinstall), the running gateway process holds ESM module caches pointing to the old hash-named dist files. Inbound messages fail silently with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` — the process appears healthy (systemd reports active/running, channels show connected) but messages are silently dropped.

This fix adds a best-effort TCP probe to `127.0.0.1:18789` during postinstall. When a gateway is detected, it prints a visible ASCII warning box suggesting `openclaw gateway restart`.

Users can suppress the warning with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_POSTINSTALL_RESTART_WARNING=true`.

Fixes #92241

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Postinstall script now warns when a gateway is running with stale module imports.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: WSL2 Ubuntu (Linux 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2), Node v22.22.2

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
$ pnpm test -- test/scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:

```
. postinstall$ node scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.mjs
. postinstall:   ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
. postinstall:   ║  WARNING: OpenClaw gateway restart recommended              ║
. postinstall:   ║  ...
. postinstall:   ║  Run:  openclaw gateway restart                              ║
. postinstall:   ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
. postinstall: Done
```

The warning appeared automatically — the postinstall detected the development gateway running on port 18789.

**Observed result after the fix**: On each `npm install -g` (or `pnpm install` triggering postinstall), if the gateway is running on the default port, users will see a clear ASCII warning suggesting restart. The check is best-effort with 1s timeout — it silently skips if the gateway isn't running or is on a non-default port.

**What was not tested**: Gateways on non-default ports, behind TLS proxies, or in containers. The TCP probe only checks 127.0.0.1:18789. A running gateway on a custom port would not trigger the warning.

## Tests and validation

39/39 tests passed (all existing + no regression):

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  39 passed (39)
   Start at  14:21:38
   Duration  1.91s
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes` / `No`)

**Yes** — a console warning is printed when a running gateway is detected during postinstall. This is the intended improvement.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes` / `No`)

**No** — existing env flags (`OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGIN_POSTINSTALL`) continue to work. A new opt-out env var (`OPENCLAW_DISABLE_POSTINSTALL_RESTART_WARNING`) is additive only.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes` / `No`)

**Minimal** — a TCP connect to 127.0.0.1:18789 with 1s timeout. No data is sent or received. The socket is destroyed immediately on successful connect. Loopback-only, no external network access.

What is the highest-risk area?
- Unexpected false positive: a non-gateway process listening on 18789 could trigger the warning. False negatives (missed gateway on non-default port) are safe — no warning is better than a wrong warning.

How is that risk mitigated?
- The warning text is advisory only ("restart recommended"), not an error. A false positive causes no harm beyond a visual suggestion. The 1s timeout prevents the postinstall from hanging.

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Maintainer review
